assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md contains: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable Library = quinn-proto, Affected Version Range = 'versions before 0.11.14 (< 0.11.14)', Fixed Version = 0.11.14 (lines 10-14 of the parsed data table).

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 16: Ecosystem = Cargo. The Ecosystem Detection section (lines 33-34) explains: 'The vulnerable library quinn-proto is a Rust crate. The Ecosystem Mappings table in security-matrix.md lists Cargo as the ecosystem for the backend repository in both the 2.1.x and 2.2.x streams.'

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lists all 7 versions from security-matrix-mock.md: Stream 2.1.x has 2.1.0 (v0.3.8) and 2.1.1 (v0.3.12); Stream 2.2.x has 2.2.0 (v0.4.5), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11), 2.2.4 (v0.4.12). Summary on line 33 confirms 'Total versions assessed: 7 (2 in 2.1.x stream, 5 in 2.2.x stream)'. This matches exactly the 7 versions in the supportability matrix.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md has a 'Pinned Tag' column showing specific tags from the supportability matrix: v0.3.8, v0.3.12 (2.1.x stream) and v0.4.5, v0.4.8, v0.4.8 (retag), v0.4.11, v0.4.12 (2.2.x stream). These match the backend column values in security-matrix-mock.md. No reference to branch HEAD or latest commits.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 26: Version 2.2.2 shows Build 0.4.9, Pinned Tag v0.4.8, quinn-proto 0.11.12, Affected = YES, Notes = 'Retag of v0.4.8; carry forward from 2.2.1'. The retag is correctly identified (tag v0.4.8 same as 2.2.1) and the affected status is carried forward.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md shows: Current state = 'Affects Versions: RHTPA 2.0.0' (line 5). Proposed Correction (lines 42-43): 'Remove: RHTPA 2.0.0 (not a valid version in the supportability matrix)' and 'Add: RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2'. The diff is clear showing what to remove and what to add.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md uses version names throughout: 'RHTPA 2.2.0', 'RHTPA 2.2.1', 'RHTPA 2.2.2' (lines 43-47). Line 49 explicitly states: 'This proposal uses Jira version names with the configured version prefix "RHTPA" as specified in the project\'s Security Configuration. The actual Jira update should match these version names to the corresponding Jira version objects in the TC project.' No numeric Jira version IDs are used.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1: Repository = 'backend' (line 19), Target Branch = 'release/0.4.z' (line 25), Acceptance Criteria section with 3 checkboxes (lines 47-49), Dependencies linking to TC-8001 (line 57). Task 2: Repository = 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (line 71), Target Branch = 'main' (line 75), Acceptance Criteria with 2 checkboxes (lines 93-94), Dependencies linking to upstream task and TC-8001 (lines 100-101). Proposed Jira Linkage section (lines 108-113) specifies Blocks dependency between downstream and upstream tasks.

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 5-6: 'Ecosystem: Cargo (source dependency)' and 'Task creation pattern: Two tasks (upstream backport + downstream propagation)'. Task 1 is titled 'Upstream Backport Task' (line 10), Task 2 is titled 'Downstream Propagation Subtask' (line 62). Proposed Jira Linkage item 3 (line 110): 'Link the downstream propagation task as blocked by the upstream backport task with type "Blocks"'.

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md line 40: 'PROPOSAL (requires confirmation before execution)'. remediation.md lines 12 and 64: both tasks labeled 'Proposed Jira Issue'. remediation.md line 114: 'All actions above are proposals pending confirmation before execution.' Proposed Jira Linkage items 4-5 (lines 111-112) use 'Propose' for status transition and assignment. No language suggests actions were actually executed.
